column1: #   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   classes and the MITRE CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) schema. OCSF
#   has a dedicated Cwe object that directly models a CWE entry. Vulnerability-
#   related classes are linked to CWE Weakness as the root-cause concept for

column8: Direct 1:1 — OCSF Cwe object models a CWE entry carrying uid/caption describing a software weakness; CWE Weakness is the canonical MITRE concept.
A vulnerability typically has one or more CWE root-cause weaknesses; the relationship is causal rather than structural equivalence.
OCSF AffectedCode describes a vulnerable code block; CWE DemonstrativeExample provides code snippets illustrating a weakness — structurally analogous.
A vulnerability finding event includes a CWE reference identifying the underlying weakness class; related rather than equivalent.
